title: carpopogon pruriens
intro: Carpopogon pruriens, also known as Black Pepper Vine or Indian Cat's Claw, is a tropical vine native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carpopogon pruriens include relieving pain and reducing inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and protecting against infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or extract, and is often used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
